form_data.Expected benefits:
Automated real-time detection of suspicious movement patterns such as route deviations, ghost shipments, circular trips, or repeated invoice usage. State-wise and sector-wise heatmaps showing goods movement behavior, trend shifts, and anomaly hotspots. Stronger compliance by identifying potential under-invoicing, over-invoicing, or fraudulent trips. Reduction in tax evasion and revenue leakage through early anomaly detection. Better departmental decision-making using ML-powered risk scoring for goods movement.

Potential Challenges: Integration with existing department
form_data.Idea Description: This system analyzes the movement of goods across states, sectors, and taxpayers using multiple datasets including e-way bills, GPS data, and GST filings (GSTR-1). The system focuses on identifying unusual or suspicious activities by: Monitoring route patterns and flagging route deviations or unexpected stops. Detecting anomalies such as ghost shipments, duplicate trips, repeated invoice use, and invoice-movement mismatches. Integrating GPS traces and e-way bill records to compute route compliance scores using ML models. Cross-verifying movement data with GSTR-1 declarations to detect discrepancies in invoices vs. actual movement. Providing dashboards with alerts, visual heatmaps, trip histories, and anomaly explanations.
